derivationSteps.description: Optional: intermediate inference steps connecting premises to conclusion. Use for multi-step proofs where a single-jump conclusion would obscure the reasoning. Omit or leave empty for one-step conclusions. Added in v0.5.2 as Enrichment 1 of the core-triad schema enrichment proposal (see docs/ROADMAP).

stepNumber.description: 1-indexed position of this step in the derivation chain. Step numbers should be sequential and unique within derivationSteps[].

premisesUsed.description: 0-indexed references into the top-level premises[] array. May be empty if this step derives only from prior steps.

stepsUsed.description: References to prior stepNumber values. Each referenced step must appear earlier in derivationSteps[] — no forward references.

intermediateConclusion.description: The fact or claim derived at this step. The last step's intermediateConclusion should match (or closely match) the top-level conclusion.

inferenceRule.description: Name of the classical inference rule applied at this step (e.g., 'modus ponens', 'modus tollens', 'hypothetical syllogism', 'disjunctive syllogism', 'universal instantiation', 'reductio ad absurdum'). Free-text in v0.5.2; may become an enum in a future release.
